title: Auditing scripts and scoring risk
text: audit_script() reads R source files line line extracts every qualified function call — one uses :: ::: namespace operator. Unqualified calls intentionally ignored. package determine package bare filter() call belongs source text alone, guessing produce false positives. Using explicit namespacing (pkg::fn) reproducibility best practice, audit_script() rewards .
code: dplyr::filter(df, x > 0)      # detected  — pkg = "dplyr", fn = "filter" filter(df, x > 0)              # not detected — package ambiguous dplyr:::internal_fn()          # detected  — pkg = "dplyr", fn = "internal_fn"

title: Auditing scripts and scoring risk
text: audit_script() resolves version every detected package one two sources, order preference: renv.lock — renv.lock file exists working directory renv = TRUE (default), versions read lockfile. gives stable, reproducible version information CI environments installed library may differ project’s declared versions. Installed library — renv.lock present (renv = FALSE), audit_script() calls installed.packages() resolve versions whatever currently installed.
code: script <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines(c(   "x <- dplyr::filter(mtcars, cyl == 4)",   "y <- ggplot2::ggplot(x, ggplot2::aes(mpg, wt))" ), script)  # renv = FALSE — use installed library (no renv.lock in tempdir) report <- audit_script(script, renv = FALSE, verbose = FALSE)  # Version column — NA means the package is not installed report$calls[, c("pkg", "fn", "pkg_version")] #>       pkg     fn pkg_version #> 1   dplyr filter        <NA> #> 2 ggplot2 ggplot        <NA> #> 3 ggplot2    aes        <NA>

title: Auditing scripts and scoring risk
text: powerful check. risk_score() looks every detected pkg::fn call internal database known cases package update silently changed function’s behaviour without producing error warning. match, checks whether installed (locked) version falls inside risk window — half-open interval (from_ver, to_ver]: version outside window flagged, even function database. avoids false positives users older newer versions specific change apply. database currently covers breaking changes : dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2, readr, purrr, stringr, lubridate, broom, data.table, lme4, base R (R 3.6.0 RNG change R 4.0.0 hclust() tie-breaking change). See contributing database vignette add new entries.
code: installed version > from_ver  AND  installed version <= to_ver          ↑                                    ↑    last "safe" version          first version where the    (not inclusive)               breaking change applies # Write a script that calls a function with a known breaking change risky_script <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines(c(   "# dplyr 1.1.0 changed summarise() grouping behaviour",   "x <- dplyr::group_by(mtcars, cyl)",   "y <- dplyr::summarise(x, mean_mpg = mean(mpg))",   "z <- stringr::str_c('a', NA)"   # str_c NA-handling changed in 1.5.0 ), risky_script)  report <- audit_script(risky_script, renv = FALSE, verbose = FALSE) risks  <- risk_score(report, methods = "changelog") print(risks) #>  #> -- reproducr risk score -- #>  #>   No risks detected. All checks passed.

title: Auditing scripts and scoring risk
text: check finds every call stochastic function verifies set.seed() call appears within 50 lines file. Stochastic functions covered: 50-line window intentional: set.seed() call top 500-line script protect stochastic call bottom, code refactored, reordered, split across files time.
code: stats::sample    stats::runif     stats::rnorm     stats::rbinom stats::rpois     stats::rexp      stats::rgamma    stats::rbeta stats::rcauchy   stats::rchisq    stats::rf        stats::rt stats::rgeom     stats::rhyper    stats::rnbinom   stats::rweibull base::sample     base::sample.int seed_script <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines(c(   "# First call — no seed above it",   "x <- stats::rnorm(100)",   "",   "# Second call — seed present within 50 lines",   "set.seed(237)",   "y <- stats::rbinom(100, 1, 0.5)",   "",   "# Third call — seed is there but 60 lines away (beyond the window)",   rep("z <- 1", 55),   "w <- stats::runif(10)" ), seed_script)  report <- audit_script(seed_script, renv = FALSE, verbose = FALSE) risks  <- risk_score(report, methods = "seed_check") as.data.frame(risks)[, c("line", "call", "risk", "description")] #>   line         call   risk #> 1    2 stats::rnorm medium #> 2   64 stats::runif medium #>                                                                                                                                         description #> 1  rnorm() is stochastic but no set.seed() was found in the 50 lines above this call (line 2). Output will differ across runs without a fixed seed. #> 2 runif() is stochastic but no set.seed() was found in the 50 lines above this call (line 64). Output will differ across runs without a fixed seed.

title: Auditing scripts and scoring risk
text: three checks run default. can select subset: Filter minimum severity min_risk:
code: full_script <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines(c(   "x <- dplyr::summarise(mtcars, n = dplyr::n())",   "y <- stats::rnorm(10)",   "z <- base::sort(letters)" ), full_script)  report <- audit_script(full_script, renv = FALSE, verbose = FALSE)  # All checks all_risks <- risk_score(report)  # Changelog only changelog_risks <- risk_score(report, methods = "changelog")  # Seed and locale only other_risks <- risk_score(report, methods = c("seed_check", "locale_check"))  nrow(all_risks) #> [1] 2 nrow(changelog_risks) #> [1] 0 nrow(other_risks) #> [1] 2 # Only items worth acting on immediately high_only <- risk_score(report, min_risk = "high")  # Medium and above medium_up <- risk_score(report, min_risk = "medium")  # Everything (default) all_items <- risk_score(report, min_risk = "low")  c(high = nrow(high_only), medium_up = nrow(medium_up), all = nrow(all_items)) #>      high medium_up       all  #>         0         1         2

title: Certifying outputs and detecting drift
text: Packages change hands. Maintainers push silent fixes. Platform-level libraries (BLAS, LAPACK) get updated system administrators. R changes RNG defaults minor versions. can alter numerical results without producing error. certify() check_drift() detect . idea simple: successful analysis run, hash key outputs store hashes. Later — change environment — re-run analysis compare new hashes stored ones. mismatch reported explicitly, output name.

title: Certifying outputs and detecting drift
text: Pass fully named list R objects want protect. Common choices:
code: model <- lm(mpg ~ wt + cyl, data = mtcars)  certify(   outputs = list(     coefs       = coef(model),     r_squared   = summary(model)$r.squared,     sigma       = sigma(model),     n_obs       = nrow(mtcars),     n_complete  = sum(complete.cases(mtcars)),     group_means = aggregate(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars, FUN = mean)   ),   tag    = "baseline-v1",   script = "analysis.R",   file   = cert_file ) #> reproducr: certified 6 output(s) [2026-06-02] under tag 'baseline-v1'

code: model2 <- lm(mpg ~ wt + cyl, data = mtcars)  result <- check_drift(   outputs = list(     coefs       = coef(model2),     r_squared   = summary(model2)$r.squared,     sigma       = sigma(model2),     n_obs       = nrow(mtcars),     n_complete  = sum(complete.cases(mtcars)),     group_means = aggregate(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars, FUN = mean)   ),   against = "baseline-v1",   file    = cert_file ) #>  #> -- reproducr drift check vs 'baseline-v1' -- #>  #>   Verdict  : ALL OUTPUTS MATCH #>   OK       : 1 #>   Drifted  : 0 #>   Missing  : 0 #>   New      : 5

title: Certifying outputs and detecting drift
code: certify(   outputs = list(     stays_same  = 42L,     will_change = coef(lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)),     will_vanish = "this output disappears next run"   ),   tag  = "four-statuses",   file = cert_file ) #> reproducr: certified 3 output(s) [2026-06-02] under tag 'four-statuses'  demo_result <- check_drift(   outputs = list(     stays_same  = 42L,     will_change = coef(lm(mpg ~ hp, data = mtcars)),     brand_new   = "this output is new"   ),   against = "four-statuses",   file    = cert_file ) #>  #> -- reproducr drift check vs 'four-statuses' -- #>  #>   Verdict  : DRIFT DETECTED #>   OK       : 1 #>   Drifted  : 1 #>   Missing  : 1 #>   New      : 1 #>  #>   Drifted outputs: #>     - will_change  print(demo_result) #>  #> -- reproducr drift report -- #>  #> [OK]      stays_same #> [DRIFT]   will_change #>             Hash mismatch (numeric tolerance check requires stored values). #> [NEW]     brand_new #>             Not present in the baseline certification. #> [MISSING] will_vanish #>             Present in baseline but not supplied to check_drift().

code: result <- check_drift(outputs = current_outputs, against = "latest")  n_drifted <- sum(result$status == "drifted") if (n_drifted > 0L) {   drifted_names <- result$output[result$status == "drifted"]   stop(sprintf(     "%d output(s) have drifted since last certification: %s",     n_drifted,     paste(drifted_names, collapse = ", ")   )) }

title: Contributing to the breaking-changes database
text: database lives R/breaking_changes_db.R named list called .BREAKING_CHANGES_DB. Keys "pkg::fn" strings. key maps list entries — one per distinct breaking change function:
code: .BREAKING_CHANGES_DB <- list(    "pkg::function_name" = list(     list(       from_version = "x.y.z",   # last version where behaviour was "safe"       to_version   = "x.y.z",   # last version where breaking change applies       risk         = "high",     # "high", "medium", or "low"       description  = "...",      # plain-English explanation       reference    = "https://..." # URL to the changelog entry     )   )  )

title: Contributing to the breaking-changes database
text: from_version to_version fields define half-open interval: user’s installed version flagged : from_version last version old behaviour still applied. Set one patch version first risky version. example, breaking change introduced 1.1.0, set from_version = "1.0.99". to_version careful judgement required. window close ecosystem moved . breaking change active risk users might realistically comparing results produced versions different sides change. entire R community moved past breaking version, flag becomes noise erodes trust tool.
code: (from_version, to_version]      ↑               ↑  exclusive        inclusive installed > from_version  AND  installed <= to_version

title: Contributing to the breaking-changes database
text: Rule 1 — Permanent package changes (e.g. dplyr 1.1.0 summarise() grouping, readr 2.0.0 backend switch): Keep window open version ceiling covers current release series. user upgrading change risk. Close window future version explicitly reverts compensates change. Rule 2 — Historical base R changes (e.g. R 3.6.0 RNG defaults, R 4.0.0 hclust() tie-breaking): Close window patch series change occurred — distant future version. 2024+, active R users R >= 4.x side R 3.6.0 RNG change. Flagging produces false positive. risk applies teams actively comparing output old new R versions. Rule 3 — doubt, prefer narrower window. missed flag better false positive. False positives cause users distrust tool ignore genuine warnings. unsure whether risk still active, check change released whether anyone using modern R stack realistically encounter .
code: # dplyr summarise — permanent change, window stays open from_version = "1.0.99", to_version   = "1.1.9" # R 3.6.0 RNG change — close window at 3.6.9, not 4.9.9 from_version = "3.5.99", to_version   = "3.6.9"    # NOT "4.9.9"  # R 4.0.0 hclust change — close window at 4.0.9 from_version = "3.6.99", to_version   = "4.0.9"    # NOT "4.9.9"

title: Contributing to the breaking-changes database
text: complete worked example. Suppose discovered broom::augment() changed name residuals column .resid .std.resid model types versions 0.7.x 0.8.0. Step 1: Verify changelog. Find relevant NEWS.md entry. Copy URL. Step 2: Determine version window. change introduced 0.8.0. last safe version 0.7.x. Set: - from_version = "0.7.99" (one patch first risky version) - to_version = "1.0.9" (last version applies, high ceiling unfixed) Step 3: Determine risk level. Code selects .resid column name augment() output silently return NA error upgrade, depending selection done. Categorise "medium" — calling pattern may break, depends code. Step 4: Write description. description : - State version change introduced - Describe old behaviour - Describe new behaviour - Explain practical consequence reproducibility Step 5: Add entry. Open R/breaking_changes_db.R add entry appropriate section:
code: "broom::augment" = list(   list(     from_version = "0.7.99",     to_version   = "1.0.9",     risk         = "medium",     description  = paste0(       "In broom 0.8.0, augment() renamed residual columns for several model ",       "types — '.resid' became '.std.resid' for standardised residuals in ",       "lm and glm objects. Code that selects '.resid' by name from augment() ",       "output will silently return NA or error after upgrading."     ),     reference = "https://broom.tidymodels.org/news/index.html"   ) ),

title: Contributing to the breaking-changes database
text: submitting, verify entry behaves correctly simulating version match: test specific version (simulating user older installation), can temporarily override pkg_version calls data frame:
code: # Temporarily add a test entry to verify the detection logic # (this is what a test in tests/testthat/ would do)  # 1. Write a script that calls the at-risk function test_script <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines("x <- dplyr::summarise(mtcars, n = dplyr::n())", test_script)  # 2. Audit it — package version will be from installed library report <- audit_script(test_script, renv = FALSE, verbose = FALSE)  # 3. Confirm the call was detected report$calls[, c("pkg", "fn", "pkg_version")] #>     pkg        fn pkg_version #> 1 dplyr summarise        <NA> #> 2 dplyr         n        <NA>  # 4. Run the changelog check risks <- risk_score(report, methods = "changelog")  # 5. If the installed version is in the risk window, it will be flagged if (nrow(risks) > 0) {   as.data.frame(risks)[, c("call", "pkg_version", "risk", "check")] } else {   cat("Installed version is outside the risk window — entry is correct,\n")   cat("but not triggered by this version.\n") } #> Installed version is outside the risk window — entry is correct, #> but not triggered by this version. # Force the version to sit inside the dplyr::summarise risk window report_forced <- report report_forced$calls$pkg_version <- as.character(report_forced$calls$pkg_version) report_forced$calls$pkg_version[report_forced$calls$pkg == "dplyr"] <- "1.1.0"  risks_forced <- risk_score(report_forced, methods = "changelog") as.data.frame(risks_forced)[, c("call", "pkg_version", "risk")] #>               call pkg_version risk #> 1 dplyr::summarise       1.1.0 high

what: Minimum test for a new entry
title: Contributing to the breaking-changes database
code: test_that("risk_score() flags mypackage::myfun in the risk window", {   f <- write_script("x <- mypackage::myfun(42)")   on.exit(unlink(f))   r <- audit_script(f, renv = FALSE, verbose = FALSE)    # Simulate a version inside the risk window   r$calls$pkg_version[r$calls$pkg == "mypackage"] <- "2.1.0"    rs <- risk_score(r, methods = "changelog")   expect_true(nrow(rs) > 0L)   expect_true(any(rs$call == "mypackage::myfun")) })  test_that("risk_score() does NOT flag mypackage::myfun outside the window", {   f <- write_script("x <- mypackage::myfun(42)")   on.exit(unlink(f))   r <- audit_script(f, renv = FALSE, verbose = FALSE)    # Simulate a version before the risk window   r$calls$pkg_version[r$calls$pkg == "mypackage"] <- "1.9.0"    rs <- risk_score(r, methods = "changelog")   myfun_risks <- rs[rs$call == "mypackage::myfun" & rs$check == "changelog", ]   expect_equal(nrow(myfun_risks), 0L) })

text: packages release new versions, database entries may become stale — to_version ceiling falls current CRAN release. check_db_staleness() detects automatically: reproducr repository runs check_db_staleness() automatically every Monday via GitHub Actions workflow opens issue stale entries found. notice stale entry, opening PR update to_version valuable contribution even without adding new entry. reviewing stale entry, ask: breaking change fixed new version? Lower remove to_version. breaking change still apply? Extend to_version new release series. ecosystem moved ? Close window described version window design principles .
code: library(reproducr)  # Check all tracked packages against CRAN report <- check_db_staleness() print(report) #> #> -- reproducr database staleness report -- #> #>   STALE:     1 #>   OK:        24 #>   UNKNOWN:   0 #> #> Stale entries: #> #>   [STALE] dplyr::summarise #>           to_version=1.1.9 | current=1.2.0 #> #> Action: review each entry and update to_version in #>         R/breaking_changes_db.R.  # Check specific packages only check_db_staleness(packages = c("dplyr", "tidyr"))  # Offline — use installed versions instead of querying CRAN check_db_staleness(source = "installed")

title: Getting started with reproducr
text: finish analysis. code runs. numbers look right. stable? Package updates change function behaviour silently. Stochastic code without fixed seed produces different results every run. Results certified last month may drift month — error warning. reproducr makes risks visible trackable via three-tier workflow: Scan & score — parse scripts assess risk Baseline & drift — certify outputs detect changes time Report & export — generate human-readable audit reports works existing setup. use renv, reproducr reads lockfile automatically. configuration required.

title: Getting started with reproducr
text: entry point audit_script(). reads R source files, extracts every qualified pkg::fn call, resolves version package use.
code: # Create a small example script script <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines(c(   "# Example analysis",   "set.seed(237)",   "x   <- dplyr::filter(mtcars, cyl == 4)",   "y   <- dplyr::summarise(x, mean_mpg = mean(mpg), n = dplyr::n())",   "fit <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = x)",   "z   <- stats::rnorm(nrow(y))",   "out <- base::sort(unique(x$gear))" ), script)  report <- audit_script(script, renv = FALSE, verbose = FALSE) print(report) #>  #> -- reproducr audit report [2026-06-02 18:11] -- #>  #>   Files scanned:     1 #>   Packages found:    3 #>   Calls detected:    5 #>   R version:         4.6.0 #>   Platform:          Linux 6.17.0-1015-azure #>   Versions from:     installed library #>  #>   Next step: risks <- risk_score(report) report$calls #>                                 file line   pkg        fn pkg_version #> 1 /tmp/Rtmp3CuosA/file1b7017d0f028.R    3 dplyr    filter        <NA> #> 2 /tmp/Rtmp3CuosA/file1b7017d0f028.R    4 dplyr summarise        <NA> #> 3 /tmp/Rtmp3CuosA/file1b7017d0f028.R    4 dplyr         n        <NA> #> 4 /tmp/Rtmp3CuosA/file1b7017d0f028.R    6 stats     rnorm       4.6.0 #> 5 /tmp/Rtmp3CuosA/file1b7017d0f028.R    7  base      sort       4.6.0

title: Getting started with reproducr
text: Pass report risk_score() run three independent checks: "changelog" — checks calls curated database known silent breaking changes "seed_check" — flags stochastic functions without nearby set.seed() "locale_check" — flags functions whose output varies system locale
code: risks <- risk_score(report) print(risks) #>  #> -- reproducr risk score -- #>  #>   HIGH:      0 #>   MEDIUM:    0 #>   LOW:       1 #>  #> [LOW]     base::sort  (line 7 in file1b7017d0f028.R) #>          Check    : locale_check #>          Details  : sort() output is locale-sensitive. Current locale: C.UTF-8. #>                     Results may differ on machines with different LC_COLLATE or #>                     LC_TIME settings. #>          Reference: https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/locales.html # High-severity only high_risks <- risk_score(report, min_risk = "high")  # Just the seed check seed_issues <- risk_score(report, methods = "seed_check") # As a plain data frame for downstream use as.data.frame(risks) #>                                 file line       call pkg_version risk #> 1 /tmp/Rtmp3CuosA/file1b7017d0f028.R    7 base::sort       4.6.0  low #>          check #> 1 locale_check #>                                                                                                                                 description #> 1 sort() output is locale-sensitive. Current locale: C.UTF-8. Results may differ on machines with different LC_COLLATE or LC_TIME settings. #>                                                              reference #> 1 https://stat.ethz.ch/R-manual/R-devel/library/base/html/locales.html

title: Generating reports and badges
text: recommended CI pattern updates badge automatically every push: workflow template also available file inside package:
code: name: Reproducibility audit  on:   push:     branches: [main, master]   schedule:     - cron: '0 6 * * 1'   # Weekly on Monday at 06:00 UTC  jobs:   audit:     runs-on: ubuntu-latest     permissions:       contents: write      steps:       - uses: actions/checkout@v4         with:           token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}        - uses: r-lib/actions/setup-r@v2         with:           r-version: 'release'           use-public-rspm: true        - name: Install reproducr         run: Rscript -e "install.packages('.', repos = NULL, type = 'source')"        - name: Run reproducibility audit         run: |           Rscript -e "             library(reproducr)             report <- audit_script('vignettes/', renv = FALSE, verbose = FALSE)             risks  <- risk_score(report)             repro_badge(report, risks, output = 'README')             n_high <- sum(risks\$risk == 'high', na.rm = TRUE)             if (n_high > 0) {               message(n_high, ' high-severity reproducibility risk(s) detected.')             }           "        - name: Commit updated badge         if: github.event_name == 'push'         run: |           git config user.name  'github-actions[bot]'           git config user.email '41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com'           git add README.md           git diff --staged --quiet || \             git commit -m 'chore: update reproducibility badge [skip ci]'           git push system.file("templates", "github_actions_audit.yml", package = "reproducr")

what: Certify analytical outputs as a reproducibility baseline — certify
title: Certify analytical outputs as a reproducibility baseline — certify
text: Hashes named list R objects (model coefficients, summary statistics, key scalars, data frames) saves alongside full environment metadata local certification file (.reproducr.rds default). Later runs can call check_drift() verify results changed. Think certify() "signed receipt" completed analysis run.

title: Certify analytical outputs as a reproducibility baseline — certify
code: certify(outputs, tag, script = NULL, file = ".reproducr")

title: Certify analytical outputs as a reproducibility baseline — certify
text: outputs fully named list R objects certify. element hashed using SHA-256 (base-R fallback digest available). Common choices: coef(model), summary(model)$r.squared, results data.frame, key scalar. tag character(1). human-readable label certification, e.g. "submission-v1" "pre-review". Tags must unique within certification file; passing duplicate tag overwrites existing record warning. script character(1) NULL. Path script produced outputs. Used documentation certification record ; validated. Default NULL. file character(1). Base path certification store. actual file written paste0(file, ".rds"). Default ".reproducr", writes .reproducr.rds current working directory. Commit file version control.

title: Certify analytical outputs as a reproducibility baseline — certify

title: Check whether breaking-changes database entries are stale — check_db_staleness
text: Compares to_version ceiling entry breaking-changes database current version package CRAN. title: Check whether breaking-changes database entries are stale — check_db_staleness
text: packages character NULL. Package names check. NULL (default), packages tracked breaking-changes database checked. verbose logical(1). Print progress messages. Default TRUE. source character(1). resolve current package versions. One : "cran" Query CRAN package database via utils::available.packages(). Requires internet connection. "installed" Use locally installed versions via utils::installed.packages(). Fast offline, reflects installed current machine. Default "cran". x staleness_report object. ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: Check whether breaking-changes database entries are stale — check_db_staleness
text: data.frame class c("staleness_report", "data.frame") one row per database entry. Columns: key pkg::fn key. pkg Package name. fn Function name. to_version ceiling version currently database. current_version current version CRAN installed. status One "ok", "stale", "unknown". gap version difference string, e.g. "1.1.9 -> 1.3.0". NA status "unknown" "ok". Rows ordered: stale first, ok, unknown. Returned invisibly.

title: Check whether breaking-changes database entries are stale — check_db_staleness
text: stale entry automatically mean database wrong. means package released new version since ceiling set. human must determine whether: breaking change still applies new version (extend ceiling). new version fixed reverted change (lower remove ceiling). entry closed ecosystem moved . See contributing vignette guidance setting to_version.

title: Check whether breaking-changes database entries are stale — check_db_staleness

what: Check analytical outputs for drift against a certified baseline — check_drift
title: Check analytical outputs for drift against a certified baseline — check_drift
text: Re-hashes set named R objects compares previously stored certification. Reports outputs unchanged ("ok"), changed ("drifted"), present baseline supplied ("missing"), new outputs baseline ("new").

title: Check analytical outputs for drift against a certified baseline — check_drift
code: check_drift(   outputs,   against = "latest",   file = ".reproducr",   tolerance = 1e-10 )

title: Check analytical outputs for drift against a certified baseline — check_drift
text: outputs fully named list current R objects — names used certify() call compared . character(1). certification tag compare . Use "latest" (default) automatically select recently added certification. file character(1). Base path certification store. Default ".reproducr" (reads .reproducr.rds). tolerance numeric(1). Numeric tolerance applied hash comparison. > 0, outputs whose hashes differ also compared element-wise (numeric vectors/matrices), flagged "ok" maximum absolute difference within tolerance. Set 0 exact matching . Default 1e-10.

title: Check analytical outputs for drift against a certified baseline — check_drift
text: Invisibly returns data.frame class c("drift_report", "data.frame") columns output, status ("ok", "drifted", "missing", "new"), max_delta, note. Also prints summary console.

title: Check analytical outputs for drift against a certified baseline — check_drift
code: cert_file <- tempfile() model <- lm(mpg ~ wt, data = mtcars)  certify(list(coefs = coef(model)), tag = "v1", file = cert_file) #> reproducr: certified 1 output(s) [2026-06-02] under tag 'v1'  # Same outputs — should report "ok" result <- check_drift(list(coefs = coef(model)),                        against = "v1", file = cert_file) #>  #> -- reproducr drift check vs 'v1' -- #>  #>   Verdict  : ALL OUTPUTS MATCH #>   OK       : 1 #>   Drifted  : 0 #>   Missing  : 0 #>   New      : 0 #>  print(result) #>  #> -- reproducr drift report -- #>  #> [OK]      coefs #>   # Different model — should report "drifted" model2 <- lm(mpg ~ hp, data = mtcars) check_drift(list(coefs = coef(model2)),              against = "v1", file = cert_file) #>  #> -- reproducr drift check vs 'v1' -- #>  #>   Verdict  : DRIFT DETECTED #>   OK       : 0 #>   Drifted  : 1 #>   Missing  : 0 #>   New      : 0 #>  #>   Drifted outputs: #>     - coefs #>

what: Generate a reproducibility status badge — repro_badge
title: Generate a reproducibility status badge — repro_badge
text: Produces shields.io Markdown badge reflecting current reproducibility status project. badge colour-coded: Green (reproducible) â risks detected. Yellow (caution) â medium-severity risks . Red (risk) â one high-severity risks drifted outputs. Grey (unknown) â risk information supplied. Can inserted automatically README.md (e.g. GitHub Actions workflow).

title: Generate a reproducibility status badge — repro_badge
code: repro_badge(   audit,   risks = NULL,   drift = NULL,   output = "markdown",   readme_path = "README.md" )

title: Generate a reproducibility status badge — repro_badge
text: audit audit_report audit_script(). risks risk_report risk_score(). Optional. drift drift_report check_drift(). Optional. output character(1). "markdown" (return badge string) "README" (insert/update badge README.md). Default "markdown". readme_path character(1). Path README file output = "README". Default "README.md".

title: Generate a reproducibility status badge — repro_badge
text: Invisibly returns badge Markdown string.

title: Generate a reproducibility status badge — repro_badge
code: script <- tempfile(fileext = ".R") writeLines("x <- dplyr::filter(mtcars, cyl == 4)", script) report <- audit_script(script, renv = FALSE, verbose = FALSE) risks  <- risk_score(report)  badge <- repro_badge(report, risks) #> [![reproducibility](https://img.shields.io/badge/reproducibility-reproducible-brightgreen)](https://reproducr-dev.github.io/reproducr/)  cat(badge) #> [![reproducibility](https://img.shields.io/badge/reproducibility-reproducible-brightgreen)](https://reproducr-dev.github.io/reproducr/)

what: Generate a human-readable reproducibility report — repro_report
title: Generate a human-readable reproducibility report — repro_report
text: Renders reproducibility audit report audit_script() result optionally risk_score() result check_drift() result. Three style presets available: "minimal" â compact summary suitable console review internal project documentation. "academic" â generates ready--paste methods paragraph journal submissions, listing packages versions summarising risk findings. "pharma" â structured QC document risk register sign-fields, suitable pharmaceutical regulated analytical workflows.

title: Generate a human-readable reproducibility report — repro_report
code: repro_report(   audit,   risks = NULL,   drift = NULL,   format = "text",   style = "minimal",   output_file = NULL )

title: Generate a human-readable reproducibility report — repro_report
text: audit audit_report object audit_script(). Required. risks risk_report data frame risk_score(). Optional strongly recommended â without , report assess reproducibility. drift drift_report data frame check_drift(). Optional. format character(1). Output format: "text" (console), "md" (Markdown file), "html" (HTML file). Default "text". style character(1). Report style: "minimal", "academic", "pharma". Default "minimal". output_file character(1) NULL. Output file path (used format = "md" format = "html"). NULL, sensible default name used ("reproducr_report.md" / "reproducr_report.html").

what: Score function calls for reproducibility risk — risk_score
title: Score function calls for reproducibility risk — risk_score
text: Takes audit_report checks every detected pkg::fn call three independent checks: "changelog" — matches curated database known breaking changes popular CRAN packages, flagging calls installed version falls known-risky version window. "seed_check" — flags stochastic functions (rnorm, sample, etc.) set.seed() appears within 50 lines call. "locale_check" — flags functions whose output locale-sensitive (sort(), format(), tolower(), etc.).

title: Score function calls for reproducibility risk — risk_score
code: risk_score(   audit,   methods = c("changelog", "seed_check", "locale_check"),   min_risk = "low" )  # S3 method for class 'risk_report' print(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'risk_report' as.data.frame(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'risk_report' x[i, j, ...]

Minimum risk level include output. One "low" (show ), "medium", "high". Default "low". x risk_report object (print, .data.frame, [). ... Additional arguments (currently unused). Row index. j Column index. columns subsetted required columns removed, "risk_report" class stripped print.risk_report() called incomplete object.

Columns: file Source file path. line Line number call. call pkg::fn string. pkg_version Installed lockfile-resolved version. risk "high", "medium", "low". check check flagged : "changelog", "seed_check", "locale_check". description Plain-English explanation risk. reference URL relevant changelog documentation. Rows ordered risk severity (high first), file line. risks found, empty data frame columns returned.

title: reproducr 0.1.1
text: Fixed audit_script(renv = TRUE) incorrectly falling back installed library valid renv.lock present. regex parser matching R version field lockfile header, causing length mismatch. Now uses jsonlite available robust JSON parsing, corrected regex fallback. Fixed audit_script() skip prose lines .Rmd .qmd files — lines inside fenced ```{r} code blocks now parsed. Previously, inline backtick references like `stats::sample()` prose incorrectly detected qualified calls, producing false positives auditing vignettes. Added check_db_staleness() — compares to_version ceilings breaking-changes database current CRAN releases. Returns tidy staleness_report data frame "ok", "stale", "unknown" status per entry. weekly GitHub Actions workflow opens issue reproducr-db automatically stale entries detected. Narrowed version windows base R RNG entries (stats::rnorm, stats::rbinom, stats::runif, stats::sample) to_version = "4.9.9" "3.6.9". Users modern R (>= 4.x) falsely flagged 2019 change side . Narrowed version window stats::hclust "4.9.9" "4.0.9" reason. Added version window design principles R/breaking_changes_db.R expanded vignette("contributing---database") three rules setting to_version quick-reference table. Launched reproducr-db — companion repository community-contributed breaking-change entries. 29 existing entries available JSON files validation CI workflow every PR. Added jsonlite Suggests support robust renv.lock parsing. Added spelling wordlist (inst/WORDLIST) — spelling errors.

title: reproducr 0.1.0
text: audit_script() — parse .R, .Rmd, .qmd files extract qualified pkg::fn calls version resolution renv.lock installed library. risk_score() — three independent risk checks: "changelog" (curated database known breaking changes), "seed_check" (flags stochastic functions without nearby set.seed()), "locale_check" (flags locale-sensitive operations). certify() — hash store analytical outputs signed baseline. check_drift() — compare current outputs stored baseline; reports "ok", "drifted", "missing", "new" statuses. list_certs() — inspect certifications stored project’s .reproducr.rds file. repro_report() — render audit reports three styles ("minimal", "academic", "pharma") three formats ("text", "md", "html"). repro_badge() — generate shields.io reproducibility status badge optionally insert README.md. Initial breaking-changes database covering dplyr, tidyr, ggplot2, readr, purrr, stringr, lubridate, broom, data.table, lme4, base R (R 3.6.0 RNG changes, R 4.0.0 hclust() tie-breaking).
